title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaw clenching

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it targets the underlying anxiety and stress that often contribute to teeth grinding. The plant's active compounds, including kavalactones, work to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of tension and promoting relaxation. As a result, individuals who use kava tincture may experience a decrease in jaw clenching and teeth grinding, leading to improved overall oral health and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: Kava's ability to calm anxiety helps alleviate muscle tension, including jaw clenching, by reducing feelings of stress and unease.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava contains compounds that can relax muscles, including those in the face and jaw, which can help reduce or eliminate jaw clenching caused by tension.

Analgesic (pain-relieving) effects: Kava's pain-relieving properties can help alleviate headaches, facial pain, and discomfort associated with chronic jaw clenching, making it a natural treatment for temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: These compounds have been shown to have a relaxing effect on muscles, which may help alleviate jaw clenching by reducing muscle tension.
Stilbenes: Stilbenes, such as isorhapontigenin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw clenching.
Furostanol saponins: Furostanol saponins, such as diosgenin, have been found to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, which may help reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Piper methysticum are commonly used due to their high concentration of kavalactones, which are known for their analgesic and muscle-relaxing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their kavalactone content, which helps to reduce muscle tension and alleviate jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aerial parts of the plant in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at a temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 1-2 weeks.
Crush the dried plant material using a mortar and pestle to release its active compounds and oils.
Combine the crushed plant material with a neutral solvent such as ethanol or glycerin in a ratio of 1:3 to 1:5 for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its unique blend of herbs that work together to ease tension in the jaw muscles. The antispasmodic properties of passionflower, a key ingredient in maypop tincture, help to relax the muscles and reduce spasms, while the adaptogenic properties of ashwagandha help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often contribute to jaw clenching. The result is a natural relief from tension headaches and facial pain caused by clenched jaws.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic Properties: Maypop tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm tense muscles, including those in the jaw, reducing spasms and pain associated with jaw clenching.
Analgesic Properties: The analgesic compounds found in maypop tinctures help to numb or reduce pain in the jaw and surrounding areas, providing relief from discomfort caused by jaw clenching.

Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: Maypop tincture's anxiolytic properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common contributors to jaw clenching, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw tension.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: This tryptophan-derived alkaloid may help with jaw clenching by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and acting as a mild sedative.
Isovitexin: A flavonoid glycoside, isovitexin may contribute to its muscle relaxant properties, helping to alleviate tension and spasms in the jaw muscles.
Flavonoids (e.g., vitexin, isoorientin): These plant-derived compounds have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and relaxing effects, which may help to reduce jaw tension and promote muscle relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and alkaloids that contribute to the plant's sedative properties, helping to relax muscles and reduce jaw tension.
Fruits: The pulp of the fruit contains a high concentration of passifloric acid, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are rich in flavonoids, which are believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, potentially helping to reduce jaw clenching and related anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves by cutting them at dawn when they are at their highest potency and freshness level.
Cleanse the harvested flowers and leaves by washing them with cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the cleaned flowers and leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper bags for 7 to 10 days.
Steep one part of dried passiflora incarnata in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture by pouring it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with jaw clenching because its calming properties can effectively soothe muscle tension in the face, jaw, and neck. The herb's sedative effects can help to relax the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), reducing the frequency and severity of clenching and grinding habits. As a natural stress reliever, valerian tinctures can also alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, further contributing to a decrease in jaw tension and discomfort. By addressing underlying causes of bruxism, valerian tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing jaw clenching and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian's calming properties help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to jaw clenching by relaxing the muscles and reducing tension.
Muscle relaxant: Valerian has a natural ability to relax muscle contractions, including those in the jaw, which can help alleviate jaw clenching and related symptoms like TMJ pain and stiffness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which can exacerbate jaw clenching and other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic Acid: This terpene helps with jaw clenching by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms in the jaw, thereby alleviating tension.
Valeranone: This sesquiterpene is believed to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related jaw clenching.
Valerenol: This sesquiterpene has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate jaw muscle activity and reduce clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the plant's sedative and muscle-relaxing properties.
Leaves: Valerian leaves contain a significant amount of valerenic acid and other active compounds, contributing to their use in tinctures for jaw clenching and anxiety relief.
Seeds: Valerian seeds, although less commonly used than roots, are also a source of valerenic acid and other active compounds, making them a potential alternative for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest clean valeriana officinalis roots in late spring or early fall when the plant is in full growth.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks or use a dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours.
Use a 1:5 ratio of dried valeriana officinalis roots to 95% ethanol or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the container every day for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 2-6 weeks of steeping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce muscle tension and stress in the face and neck, which can contribute to jaw clenching and bruxism. As a natural anxiolytic, lavender tincture helps to soothe anxiety and promote relaxation, allowing individuals to release physical tension and relax their jaw muscles, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: English lavender tincture helps to calm anxiety and stress, which is often a major contributor to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system also help to relax tense muscles in the face and jaw, reducing symptoms of bruxism (jaw clenching).

Inflammation-reducing properties: English lavender tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw tension and clenching.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with jaw clenching by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension in the jaw and face, thereby reducing symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene contributes to the muscle-relaxing and anti-anxiety properties of Lavandula angustifolia, which can help alleviate jaw clenching and other symptoms associated with stress and anxiety.
Lavandulol: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension in the jaw, leading to a decrease in jaw clenching and associated pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used in tinctures to help with jaw clenching due to their calming and relaxing properties.
Buds: The buds of Lavandula angustifolia are used in tinctures to help with jaw clenching due to their ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used in tinctures to help with jaw clenching due to their calming and soothing effects on the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers during their peak bloom in the summer months.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm area or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried flowers with 1000 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it daily for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its ability to calm the mind and nervous system. The herb's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing mental tension, Brahmi tinctures can help alleviate physical symptoms such as teeth grinding and jaw pain. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe irritated muscles and joints in the face and neck, further reducing discomfort and stiffness associated with chronic jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Brahmi tincture's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of jaw clenching and tension headaches.

Neuroprotective and antispasmodic properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds, such as brahmine and amalaki, have been shown to relax muscle spasms and reduce pain in the jaw, reducing symptoms of jaw clenching and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The anxiolytic and sedative compounds present in Brahmi tincture, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help calm the nervous system, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation of the jaw muscles, which can alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides, a group of saponins, have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Bacoside A: Bacoside A has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, both of which can help alleviate jaw clenching.
Bacopa alkaloids: Bacopa alkaloids have been found to have a sedative effect, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension in the jaw, thus helping to alleviate jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of bacosides, which are believed to reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Roots: Utilized because they contain a rich mix of bioactive compounds, including bacosides, that help alleviate stress and anxiety.
Stems: Employed for their bacoside content, which may help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200 grams of freshly dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine the cleaned bacopa monnieri material with 500ml of 80% ethanol or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks or until the liquid becomes dark brown in color.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Label and store the bacopa monnieri tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or use it immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it possesses adaptogenic properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety, two primary triggers of jaw tension. The herb's ability to calm the nervous system can relax the facial muscles, releasing tension in the jaw and alleviating symptoms of bruxism, also known as teeth grinding or clenching. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe any resulting pain and discomfort, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: The herb contains compounds that have a natural muscle-relaxing effect, which can help to ease tension in the jaw muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which may contribute to jaw pain and clenching.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate jaw clenching and related tension by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the jaw muscles.

Withaferin A: This alkaloid has been shown to possess potent anti-anxiety and anti-stress effects, which may help manage stress-related jaw clenching by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation.

Ashwagandhanso: These phenolic compounds have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the jaw muscles from oxidative damage and inflammation, thereby reducing jaw clenching and related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of bioactive compounds, including withanolides, which contribute to their therapeutic effects.
Leaves: They contain a similar set of bioactive compounds as the roots, including withanolides, which may help alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
Seeds: They are a rich source of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce jaw clenching and related pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried withania somnifera root and 750ml of 80 proof vodka for tincture preparation.
Combine the dried root with 1 cup of vodka in a clean glass jar to create a mixture.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container to separate the liquid.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to teeth grinding and clenching. The herbal remedy contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with jaw clenching, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable oral structure.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Chamomile helps to calm the mind and body, reducing tension and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Sedative effects: The sedative properties of chamomile can help to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing spasms and clenching associated with teeth grinding or bruxism.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce pain and inflammation caused by chronic jaw clenching, which can lead to conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a muscle relaxant, helping to reduce muscle tension in the jaw and alleviate jaw clenching.
Apiole: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties, which contribute to its ability to ease jaw tension and clenching.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, helping to calm the jaw muscles and reduce clenching.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain apigenin and luteolin, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce muscle tension associated with jaw clenching.
Leaves: They have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate jaw pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
Seeds: They contain chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to relax muscles and reduce pain in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers completely using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried flowers with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it contains menthol, a natural analgesic that relaxes tense muscles. When taken internally as a tincture, peppermint oil can ease jaw tension by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint can help distract from pain and discomfort, providing quick relief from jaw clenching caused by stress, anxiety, or dental issues. As a result, herbal peppermint tinctures offer a natural and effective way to calm jaw tension and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Digestive-soothing compounds: Peppermint's menthol and menthone content helps to relax digestive muscles, which can also calm tense jaw muscles and reduce clenching.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpenoid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce muscle tension and inflammation in the jaw, alleviating jaw clenching symptoms.

Menthol: This terpene has analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties, which can help relax muscles, reduce pain and inflammation, and improve blood flow to the jaw area, alleviating jaw clenching symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol, which acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory to relieve jaw pain.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are also utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relaxation, helping to alleviate tension in the jaw muscles.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita, like the leaves, contain menthol, which helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation, providing relief from jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage with paper towels.
Combine the dried leaves with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which work together to soothe tense muscles and reduce anxiety and stress. When we're feeling anxious or overwhelmed, it's common for us to subconsciously clench our jaws, but lemon balm tinctures can help calm the nervous system and alleviate this tension, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Relaxation and sedative effects: Herbal lemon balm tincture helps to relax the muscles involved in jaw clenching, including the masseter, temporalis, and medial pterygoid muscles, thereby reducing tension and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tincture's antispasmodic effects help to alleviate muscle spasms and cramps associated with jaw clenching, providing relief from pain and stiffness.

Anti-anxiety benefits: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties of lemon balm tincture can contribute to a decrease in stress and anxiety levels, which are common triggers for jaw clenching disorders such as bruxism, thereby reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-anxiety and muscle relaxant properties, which can help alleviate jaw clenching caused by stress and anxiety.
Glycosides: Specifically, melitoside and verbascoside, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, potentially reducing muscle tension and jaw clenching.
Linalool: A terpene with a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, thereby decreasing the likelihood of jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Stems: The stems are used to create a tincture that can help relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make a tincture that can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly by spreading it on paper towels for 1 week.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with 70 vodka or 25 glycerin as solvent in a 1 to 5 ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep in a clean glass jar for 2 to 4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid tincture from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it contains a compound called baicalin, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. When taken as a supplement, skullcap tincture can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of jaw clenching. Additionally, skullcap's sedative properties can help relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with bruxism. By promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body, herbal skullcap tinctures offer a natural solution for alleviating jaw clenching symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) properties: Skullcap's anxiolytic properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: Skullcap's muscle relaxant properties help to reduce muscle spasms in the face and jaw, relieving tension and discomfort caused by chronic teeth grinding or clenching.

Adaptogenic properties: Skullcap's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress and anxiety, reducing the physical symptoms of stress (such as jaw clenching) and promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Bartogenic acid: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which may help reduce muscle tension and alleviate jaw clenching associated with anxiety and stress.

Bartosin: A flavonoid glycoside with demonstrated anxiolytic and muscle relaxant properties, bartosin may help calm the mind and body, reducing the likelihood of jaw clenching and related muscle strain.

Oleuropein: A secoiridoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, oleuropein may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to jaw clenching and associated pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as baicalein and baicalin, which have anti-anxiety and muscle-relaxing properties.
Flowers: They are often used for their flavonoids, such as baicalein and baicalin, which have anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing effects, helping to ease jaw clenching.
Roots: They are traditionally used due to their rich content of active compounds like baicalein and baicalin, which may help to reduce anxiety and muscle tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scutellaria lateriflora flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency for maximum effectiveness.
Dry the harvested flowers completely using a dehydrator on a low setting for 8 hours or by air drying.
Combine 250 grams of dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers with 750 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its natural calming properties that soothe muscle tension in the face and neck. The bitter compounds found in hops, such as lupulone and humulone, have a relaxing effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety that often contributes to jaw clenching. As these muscles relax, the constant grinding and clenching of the teeth decreases, providing relief from tension headaches, migraines, and facial pain. Regular use of herbal hops tinctures can also promote better sleep quality and overall relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Hops tincture's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching, allowing the muscles to relax and reducing tension.

Muscle-relaxant properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in hops have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help relax the muscles of the face, including the masseter muscle responsible for jaw clenching.
Sedative properties: Hops' sedative effects can promote relaxation and calmness, reducing the tendency to clench or grind one's teeth due to stress, anxiety, or other emotional factors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant contain a high concentration of bitter compounds that help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, making them effective for alleviating jaw clenching.

Buds: Hops buds are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the facial tissues and alleviate the discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hop cones from humulus lupulus plants, ensuring optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested material, removing any stems or debris thoroughly.
Combine the clean hop cones with 200ml of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a glass jar, filling it to the brim.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the muscles in the face and jaw. The herb's sedative properties work to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of bruxism or teeth grinding and clenching. As the body's tension releases, the jaw becomes more relaxed, reducing the incidence of clenching and grinding, and allowing for a smoother, more comfortable bite. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Anxiolytic Properties: St John's Wort tincture has adaptogenic and anxiolytic properties, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to jaw clenching, allowing for a more relaxed jaw and reduced teeth grinding.

Pain Relieving and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The tincture's pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, can help alleviate muscle tension and reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles, leading to relief from clenching and grinding.

Muscle Relaxant Properties: St John's Wort's flavonoids, particularly quercetin, exhibit muscle-relaxant properties that can help ease spasms and relax tense jaw muscles, reducing the likelihood of clenching and grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are valued for their ability to relax tense muscles and reduce pain.
Buds: The buds contain high concentrations of hypericin, a potent antioxidant that helps to soothe irritated tissues and calm the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency and quality.
Wash the harvested material thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the clean hypericum perforatum into small pieces and combine it with 80-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the hypericum to infuse its active compounds into the tincture.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after 48 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its unique blend of calming properties. The herbal extract contains a high concentration of citral, an essential oil that has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. When taken as a tincture, lemon verbena relaxes the facial muscles, including those in the jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with clenching. Anti-inflammatory: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, a common cause of jaw clenching, thereby providing relief from discomfort and stiffness.

Anxiolytic: The anxiolytic properties of lemon verbena can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching, allowing for more relaxed facial muscles and reduced tension.

Barks: The barks of the lemon verbena plant are rich in tannins, which provide a soothing and calming effect on the muscles of the face, helping to reduce clenching and grinding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of aloysia citriodora in a clean and dry environment.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped aloysia citriodora with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a dark glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool place for four weeks, shaking the container daily during that period.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean container, discarding the solids.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching.
Buds: The buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the jaw and facial tissues affected by clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 ounces of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5 by weight for a total of 120 ml of mixture.
Store the tincture in an airtight glass container at room temperature for eight weeks to allow for percolation and extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label with the date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its natural ability to relax muscles and reduce inflammation. The herbal compound, parthenolide, has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate tension and stress that often contributes to jaw clenching. Additionally, feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By taking an herbal feverfew tincture regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in jaw clenching and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it effective in alleviating jaw tension and clenching.
Antispasmodic properties: Feverfew's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw, reducing spasms and tension that can contribute to jaw clenching.

Muscle-relaxant and sedative compounds: Feverfew contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw pain and tension.
Flowers: The flowers contain parthenolide, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in a clean glass container.
Combine the dried herb with 4 cups of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a dark glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow maceration.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label accurately storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with jaw clenching because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce tension in the facial muscles. The tannins present in dandelion also have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate anxiety and stress that often contributes to jaw clenching. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can help to reduce water retention and swelling in the face, further alleviating tension in the jaw muscles. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal dandelion tinctures provide a natural solution for managing jaw clenching and related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw clenching, making it easier to relax the muscles and alleviate tension.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of dandelion tincture can help calm muscle spasms in the jaw, reducing the frequency and intensity of clenching episodes.

Calming and sedative: Dandelion tincture's calming and sedative effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing tension in the jaw muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, making them a great choice for relieving jaw clenching.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant are rich in bioactive compounds that can help to calm and relax the muscles of the face and jaw, providing relief from tension and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion roots approximately 500 grams.
Chop the root material into small pieces ensuring uniform size for optimal extraction.
Combine the chopped root with 95% vodka or grain alcohol in a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar at room temperature for 6-8 weeks shaking daily allowing for slow extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or coffee filters pressing out any remaining liquid from solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its natural ability to relax and calm the muscles. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce tension in the jaw, neck, and facial areas, alleviating symptoms of temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) and teeth grinding. Yarrow's flavonoids also possess anxiolytic effects, soothing anxiety and stress that may contribute to clenching habits. As a result, regular use of yarrow tinctures can promote relaxation and ease jaw tension, leading to a healthier and more comfortable jaw alignment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture helps reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to pain and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The active compounds in yarrow tincture, such as chromene and flavonoids, have been shown to relax muscle spasms and tension, including those affecting the jaw, reducing symptoms of jaw clenching.
Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow tincture's ability to prevent or relieve spasms can help alleviate tension and tightness in the jaw muscles, which are common causes of jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots are used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing jaw pain and clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves at dawn or early morning in a clean container.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and combine with vodka 25% w/w ratio.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to macerate for 14 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.
Label and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and tension in the jaw muscles. The spicy compounds in ginger also stimulate blood flow to the affected area, relaxing the muscles and reducing stiffness. Additionally, ginger's warming sensation can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to release physical tension and relax the jaw. By addressing the underlying causes of jaw clenching, ginger tinctures can bring relief from discomfort, pain, and even migraines caused by TMJ disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and joints caused by clenching.

Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects of ginger tincture can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching, providing relief for temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder sufferers.
Muscle relaxant properties: Ginger's muscle-relaxant compounds, such as 6-gingerol, can help ease tension in the jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of clenching episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to relax and soothe muscle tension, making them an effective ingredient for reducing jaw clenching.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root weighing about 2 pounds and wash it thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned ginger into small pieces measuring approximately 1 inch each size.
Combine chopped ginger with 100 proof vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar filling the jar to 75% capacity.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily ensuring complete extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaw clenching are liquid extracts made from herbs that help to relax and soothe the jaw muscles, thereby reducing the occurrence of jaw clenching. These tinctures work by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation, making them an effective natural remedy for this condition. Some of the best herbal tinctures for jaw clenching include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, Melissa officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, and Scutellaria lateriflora. If left untreated, jaw clenching can lead to chronic pain, migraines, and even affect daily life, causing stress, anxiety, and difficulty sleeping.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal tinctures are anxiety, stress-related disorders, and muscle tension. Herbs such as valerian root, passionflower, and chamomile have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive muscles like jaw clenching. Other herbs like skullcap and lavender can also be used to soothe digestive issues related to stress, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with jaw clenching is a blend of Passionflower, Kava, and Valerian root. Passionflower helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, while Kava relaxes the muscles and reduces tension. Valerian root promotes deep relaxation and improves sleep quality, which can contribute to jaw clenching at night. Taking a 20-30 drop tincture of this blend before bed can help alleviate jaw clenching and promote a restful night's sleep.

By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, Valeriana officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching and promote overall well-being.
